§6ME Conversion Monitor§f:
The §6ME Conversion Monitor §f(appliedenergistics2:part:420) is a §ecable part§f that §bdisplays the ratio of in-progress crafting jobs§f. It shows the §6amount requested §f versus the §6amount completed §f for the configured item.

§6Production Path (network/parts/monitors_conversion.json)§f:
Shapeless recipe with 3 ingredients:
- 1× §6Formation Core §f(appliedenergistics2:material:43)
- 1× §6ME Storage Monitor §f(appliedenergistics2:part:400)
- 1× §6Annihilation Core §f(appliedenergistics2:material:44)
- Yields 1× ME Conversion Monitor

§6Function (PartConversionMonitor.java)§f:
- §bCrafting ratio display §f: shows the §6requested §f vs §6completed §f count for the configured item
- §bAuto-refresh §f: updates every tick based on the §6ICraftingGrid §f state of the network
- §bUnderlying Storage Monitor §f: a Conversion Monitor §6inherits §f the storage monitoring function of the part it is built from
- §bNo config manager §f: this is a §6display-only §f part

§6Direct Descendants (TaskRetrieval graph)§f:
The jei-graph shows 0 items the ME Conversion Monitor is a component of, but it is referenced in 12 §6use sites §f across the mod pack. Common uses:
- §bCrafting job visualization §f: see how many items of a specific type are being processed
- §bBottleneck detection §f: when a crafting job stalls, the ratio display stops changing

§6Placement§f:
- §eTip§f: place a Conversion Monitor on a §6Crafting CPU §f or near a §6Pattern Provider §fface for live crafting feedback
- §eTip§f: the panel shows "0/0" when no crafting job is active
- §cWarning§f: a Conversion Monitor §cdoes not §fmonitor the §6power draw §f of the crafting job — use a §6Controller §f status screen for that
- §cWarning§f: the Conversion Monitor's display is based on the §6most recent §f crafting job for the configured item; if a §bnew job §f starts, the counter resets to the new totals
